title: Dashboard Terminal State Reconciliation
subtitle: Stop stale running state from outliving terminal lifecycle and unit evidence.
scope: Standalone living-doc harness dashboard state reconciliation

parentEpic.title: Epic: fresh realistic harness hardening run

testCaseIssue.title: Hardening: create and run a fresh realistic living-doc test case

objective: Fix the standalone harness dashboard so terminal lifecycle and inference-unit evidence always wins over stale running state. When a lifecycle-result, stop verdict, selected-unit result, repair result, closure review, or stale-supervisor artifact proves that a lifecycle or unit is no longer running, the run list, lifecycle graph, card status, selected side pane, and WebSocket/reload reconstruction must show the terminal or stale state for that exact instance instead of reusing a generic running card or an old active snapshot. The fix must be source-backed in the dashboard server or dashboard HTML, proven by focused contract/UI tests, reflected in this living doc, rendered to HTML, and safe to run through the standalone lifecycle harness with PR-review policy enabled.
successCondition: A reproduced stale-running fixture fails before the source fix and passes after it: the dashboard API and frontend graph prefer terminal result artifacts over active-lifecycle running snapshots, keep each inference unit as an instance with its own terminal status, show stale-supervisor state only when no terminal lifecycle result exists, keep selected detail panes aligned with the clicked instance or edge, and reconstruct the same non-running graph after reload or event replay. The standalone harness run for this doc either closes with contract-bound proof and PR-review evidence, or creates a blocker issue that names the exact lifecycle run, contract, raw-log, and artifact paths that prevented closure.

in: Dashboard server lifecycle list and graph artifact derivation.
Dashboard frontend graph card status and selected side pane reconciliation.
WebSocket/history reload behavior for terminal and stale lifecycle states.
Focused contract/UI tests that reproduce terminal-result precedence and per-instance unit status.
Living doc update, render, and standalone harness run evidence.

runState.nextObjectiveAction: Start the standalone lifecycle controller on this living doc with the local harness tool profile and required-before-closure PR-review policy. The first worker must inspect the dashboard server/frontend state derivation before editing.

proofRoutes.description: Proves dashboard API state derivation for terminal lifecycle results, stale active snapshots, and per-instance unit status.
Proves graph construction from local lifecycle artifacts rather than client memory.
Proves the embedded dashboard UI has the runtime hooks needed for instance-level card and side-pane status.
Proves live event and persisted history reconstruction do not resurrect stale running state.

supervisorMay: inspect raw JSONL and result artifacts
create blocker tickets from run evidence
supervisorMustNot: manually mark this doc closed outside the harness chain
treat a rendered HTML page as source-system completion
ignore raw inference logs when the run blocks
reuse a previous PR-policy run as proof for this objective

data.name: Ready for standalone harness run
data.summary: This doc is intentionally open. It defines a real dashboard state-reconciliation objective that requires source changes or proof that the source already satisfies the stricter terminal-result precedence behavior.
data.nextStep: Run through the standalone lifecycle controller; do not implement from the supervising session unless the harness exposes a blocker and the user explicitly authorizes a fix.

data.name: Terminal lifecycle result beats active running snapshot
data.objectiveTerm: terminal lifecycle evidence always wins over stale running state
data.criterion: When a lifecycle directory contains both active-lifecycle.json with running state and lifecycle-result.json with closed, blocked, stopped, or another terminal finalState, the run list and graph use the terminal lifecycle-result state.
data.proofRequired: Contract fixture writes both active and terminal artifacts and asserts finalState, controller card, graph terminal node, and run list status are terminal, not running.

data.name: Stale-supervisor is only used when terminal result is absent
data.objectiveTerm: stale-supervisor artifact proves that a lifecycle or unit is no longer running
data.criterion: The dashboard reports stale-active-lifecycle only when active-lifecycle.json claims a running supervisor and no terminal lifecycle-result.json is available; a terminal result suppresses the stale fallback.
data.proofRequired: Contract fixture covers both cases: missing terminal result produces stale-active-lifecycle; present terminal result produces the terminal result state.

data.name: Each graph card is an instance with its own status
data.objectiveTerm: for that exact instance instead of reusing a generic running card
data.criterion: Graph nodes for worker, reviewer, repair, PR-review, proof, closure, and blocker units carry instance-specific IDs, iteration or sequence metadata, status, artifact paths, and validation/log availability derived from their own result artifacts.
data.proofRequired: Artifact graph tests with multiple iterations prove an old unit remains terminal while a later unit may be active, without reusing a card status across instances.

data.name: Frontend does not resurrect stale running cards
data.objectiveTerm: run list, lifecycle graph, card status, selected side pane, and WebSocket/reload reconstruction must show the terminal or stale state
data.criterion: The dashboard UI applies streamed or reloaded terminal graph state to the matching instance card and does not leave a prior running badge visible after terminal evidence arrives.
data.proofRequired: UI contract simulates running then terminal event/history and asserts the same node changes to terminal without duplicating or reusing stale state.

data.name: Selected details follow the clicked instance or edge
data.criterion: Clicking an instance card or contract edge shows details for that exact node or edge, including status, contract label, artifact paths, raw-log availability, and commit or living-doc-change evidence when present.
data.proofRequired: UI contract or dashboard screenshot from a fixture graph demonstrates selected node and selected edge details remain distinct.

data.name: Reload reconstructs terminal state from artifacts
data.criterion: After a page reload, the dashboard reconstructs terminal graph and detail state from persisted lifecycle artifacts and event history instead of showing default running state until a new WebSocket event arrives.
data.proofRequired: Realtime UI contract loads persisted history and asserts terminal lifecycle/unit status is present before live events are emitted.

data.name: Fix is source-backed and rendered
data.objectiveTerm: source-backed in the dashboard server or dashboard HTML, proven by focused contract/UI tests, reflected in this living doc, rendered to HTML
data.criterion: The final change includes either source changes to dashboard server/dashboard HTML or explicit proof that the current source already satisfies the stricter behavior, updates this living doc with evidence, and renders the HTML.
data.proofRequired: Git diff, passing proof routes, updated living doc JSON, rendered HTML, and commit intent evidence if files changed.

data.name: Standalone harness produces closed or traceable blocker result
data.objectiveTerm: safe to run through the standalone lifecycle harness with PR-review policy enabled
data.criterion: The standalone harness run for this doc ends in closed with closure-review approval and PR-review evidence, or terminal blocked with a GitHub issue that cites the exact lifecycle run, contract, raw-log, and artifact paths.
data.proofRequired: lifecycle-result.json, reviewer verdict, closure-review or blocker result, PR-review gate evidence, and post-flight narrative if available.
data.acceptanceTest: Run via scripts/living-doc-harness-lifecycle.mjs or dashboard API with prReviewPolicy.mode=required-before-closure

data.reason: Owns graph card rendering, selected node/edge details, running badges, reload behavior, and live WebSocket updates.

Hardening: create and run a fresh realistic living-doc test case
covers: this living doc and its standalone run

data.name: Hardening: improve dashboard observability for routing decisions
covers: selected side pane and graph observability

data.name: Terminal-result precedence
data.claim: Terminal lifecycle-result evidence must override active-lifecycle running snapshots because the result is the durable controller outcome.
data.semanticRole: Precedence rule for dashboard state derivation.
data.validationQuestion: Can a fixture with contradictory active and terminal artifacts prove the dashboard chooses terminal state?
data.driftRisk: The dashboard can look live while telling the operator a false running story.

diagrams.title: Terminal Result Precedence
diagrams.text:
flowchart LR
  Active["active-lifecycle.json says running"] --> Reconcile["dashboard state reconciler"]
  Result["lifecycle-result.json says terminal"] --> Reconcile
  Supervisor["supervisor pid missing"] --> Reconcile
  Reconcile -->|result exists| Terminal["show terminal state"]
  Reconcile -->|no result and pid dead| Stale["show stale-active-lifecycle"]
  Reconcile -->|running and pid alive| Running["show running"]

data.name: Inference unit instance status
data.claim: A graph card represents a unit instance, not a reusable unit type; status belongs to the instance artifact.
data.validationQuestion: Can multiple iterations show old units as terminal while the current unit is running?
data.driftRisk: A reused card can make an old reviewer, balance scan, or repair unit appear to still be running.

diagrams.title: Instance Status Flow
diagrams.text:
flowchart LR
  UnitResult["unit result artifact"] --> Node["graph node instance"]
  Node --> Card["card status badge"]
  Node --> Pane["selected side pane"]
  EdgeContract["handoff contract"] --> Edge["graph edge label"]
  Edge --> Pane
  NewIteration["later iteration"] --> NewNode["new node instance"]
  Node -. "does not overwrite" .-> NewNode

data.currentCoverage: Existing dashboard server tests cover active and stale lifecycle states; this objective requires stricter terminal-result precedence coverage.
data.nextStep: Add or confirm a fixture where active-lifecycle is running while lifecycle-result is terminal.
gaps: Need proof that terminal result suppresses stale/running fallback.

gaps: Need proof that labels, cards, and side pane follow instance identity.

data.name: Reproduce stale-running contradiction
data.claim: A deterministic fixture can represent the bug class: active state says running while terminal artifacts prove otherwise.
data.evidenceRequired: Failing or newly added contract fixture that would fail if running state wins.
data.closureRelevance: Prevents a vague dashboard polish task from replacing the concrete state bug.

data.name: Source fix or source proof
data.claim: The source system now enforces terminal-result precedence and instance-specific status.
data.evidenceRequired: Dashboard server/HTML diff or proof that existing source already passes the stricter fixture.

data.name: Harness terminal proof
data.claim: The standalone harness can drive this fresh realistic doc to closed or a traceable blocker.
data.evidenceRequired: Lifecycle result, reviewer verdict, PR-review evidence, closure-review or blocker result, and raw-log references.
data.closureRelevance: Final proof for this test doc and issue #280.
